sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाऽहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च । शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥
hindiTranslation: क्योंकि ब्रह्म, अविनाशी अमृत, शाश्वत धर्म और ऐकान्तिक सुखका आश्रय मैं ही हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: For I am the foundation of Brahman, of immortality, of the imperishable, of eternal dharma, and of unending bliss.
essence: All ultimate freedom rests in Krishna, not apart from him.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he is the support behind Brahman, immortality, the imperishable, eternal dharma, and ultimate bliss.
simpleInsight: What you seek at the deepest level is not separate from its source.
detailedMeaning: After describing the one who rises beyond the three gunas, Krishna gives the final summit of the chapter. On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna still needing a way beyond inner bondage, Krishna says that the highest reality, lasting freedom, and ultimate fulfilment all rest in him.
modernRelevance: You finally reach the edge of a decision you cannot undo: leave the safe life, tell the hard truth, or stop pretending. This verse says the deepest security is not in control, but in what supports all reality.
translationLiteral: ब्रह्मणः (of Brahman) / हि (indeed) / प्रतिष्ठा (foundation, support) / अहम् (I am) / अमृतस्य (of immortality) / अव्ययस्य (of the imperishable) / च (and) / शाश्वतस्य (of the eternal) / च (and) / धर्मस्य (of dharma) / सुखस्य (of bliss) / ऐकान्तिकस्य (of exclusive, ultimate) / च (and)
insight: It shifts the mind from chasing separate spiritual goals to recognising their source as one.

quoteVariants.text: I am the ground of immortality, eternal dharma, and lasting bliss.
I am the ground beneath all freedom.
What never ends rests in me.
